titleStmt.title: Letter from Andrew Philip Magill to William O'Brien, 28 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew Philip Magill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter was written by Andrew Philip Magill to William O'Brien , the president of the Dublin trades council. Magill (1871 - 1941) was a civil servant and held the position of private secretary for successive chief secretaries of Ireland until his transfer to the Northern Irish Civil Service in 1921.
O'Brien (1881 - 1968) was an active labour leader and trade union official. Although O'Brien did not take part in the Easter Rising of 1916 he was closely involved in its planning. and was interned in Frongoch for a month after its outbreak. Later in his career he became a Labour Party TD for Tipperary.
In this letter Magill acknowledges the receipt of a letter previously sent by O'Brien in which he enclosed a resolution concerning the control of shipping.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3733/3

correspAction.placeName: Trade Hall, Capel Street, Dublin, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
28th February, 1916. Dear Sir, I am desired by Mr. Birrell to acknowledge the
receipt of your letter of the 26th instant forwarding
copy of a resolution adopted by the Dublin United
Trades Council and Labour League relative to the control
of shipping Yours faithfully, (Sd.) A.P.M. The Acting Secretary,
Dublin United Trades Council And Labour League,
Trades Hall,
Capel Street,
DUBLIN.
